articles.headline: Major Anti-Maoist Operation in Abujhmarh, Chhattisgarh; 27 Naxalites Killed
articles.explainer:
• A significant anti-Naxal operation in the Abujhmarh region of Narayanpur district, Chhattisgarh, resulted in the killing of 27 Naxalites.
• The operation was a joint effort by security forces, including the District Reserve Guard (DRG) and Special Task Force (STF).
• Abujhmarh is known as a Maoist stronghold and a challenging terrain for security forces due to its dense forests and lack of infrastructure.
• This incident marks one of the largest single-day casualties for Naxalites in recent times, indicating intensified efforts by the state to curb left-wing extremism.
• The operation is part of the ongoing strategy to dismantle Maoist networks and establish state control in previously inaccessible areas.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Abujhmarh is located in Narayanpur district, Chhattisgarh.
DRG (District Reserve Guard) and STF (Special Task Force) are key forces involved in anti-Naxal operations.
Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) is a significant internal security challenge in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the effectiveness of security-centric approaches versus development-centric approaches in combating LWE.
Analyze the geographical challenges and strategic importance of regions like Abujhmarh in the context of LWE.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to the persistence of LWE and government strategies to address them.
articles.imageDescription: Security forces conducting operations in a dense forest area of Chhattisgarh.
articles.trivia: Abujhmarh, literally meaning 'the unknown hills', is one of the last remaining unmapped and uncharted areas in India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Linkages between development and spread of extremism

articles.headline: Top Maoist Leader Nambala Keshava Rao, General Secretary of CPI (Maoist), Passes Away
articles.explainer:
• Nambala Keshava Rao, also known as Basavaraj or Ganesh, the General Secretary of the outlawed Communist Party of India (Maoist), has passed away at the age of 69-70.
• He assumed the leadership of the CPI (Maoist) in 2018, succeeding Ganapathy, and was considered a key strategist and military planner for the outfit.
• Rao was a former engineer and was known for his expertise in designing and manufacturing improvised explosive devices (IEDs).
• His death is expected to create a significant leadership vacuum within the top echelons of the Maoist organization.
• Security agencies view this as a potential setback for the LWE movement, possibly impacting its operational capabilities and future strategies.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: Nambala Keshava Rao was the General Secretary of the CPI (Maoist).
He was also known by the aliases Basavaraj and Ganesh.
He succeeded Ganapathy as the head of the outfit in 2018.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential impact of leadership changes on the operational effectiveness and ideological direction of LWE groups.
Discuss the role of key leaders in sustaining and expanding Naxalite movements.
Examine the challenges faced by security agencies in identifying and neutralizing top Maoist leadership.
articles.imageDescription: A blurred file photo of Nambala Keshava Rao.
articles.trivia: Nambala Keshava Rao was reportedly instrumental in modernizing the Maoists' arsenal and tactics, particularly in the use of explosives.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Linkages between development and spread of extremism

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutionality of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• Several petitions had challenged the law on grounds of privacy concerns, state surveillance powers, and potential impact on freedom of speech.
• The Court ruled that the law strikes a reasonable balance between an individual's right to privacy and legitimate state interests, including national security and public order.
• Key provisions regarding cross-border data transfers and the establishment of the Data Protection Board were specifically scrutinized and affirmed.
• This landmark judgment provides legal certainty for the implementation of India's comprehensive data protection framework.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability
Citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, is India's comprehensive data protection law.
The Supreme Court exercises powers of judicial review over parliamentary legislation.
The Right to Privacy is protected under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the balance between individual privacy rights and state interests in the context of the DPDP Act, 2023.
Discuss the implications of the DPDP Act for businesses and technology companies operating in India.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in upholding fundamental rights while also acknowledging the need for national security and public order.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of digital data flowing securely with a Supreme Court emblem in the background.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, laying the groundwork for the DPDP Act.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and data protection

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Cautious Stance on Inflation
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the eighth consecutive time.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to bringing inflation within the target band of 4% while supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and domestic food price pressures as key risks to the inflation outlook.
• The central bank reiterated its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating that future rate cuts are not imminent.
• Analysts expect the RBI to remain watchful of evolving economic indicators, particularly monsoon performance and crude oil prices, before considering any policy changes.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: The Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the policy interest rates.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth in the current global and domestic environment.
Analyze the impact of an unchanged repo rate on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in managing liquidity and inflationary pressures.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai with currency notes in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Tools and objectives of monetary policy

articles.headline: 7th India-Africa Forum Summit Concludes, Focus on Enhanced Trade and Development Partnerships
articles.explainer:
• The 7th India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) concluded today, emphasizing deeper collaboration in trade, investment, technology transfer, and capacity building.
• Leaders from India and 54 African nations discussed strategies for sustainable development, food security, climate action, and health cooperation.
• India pledged increased Lines of Credit and grants for development projects across Africa, reinforcing its 'Global South' outreach.
• A joint declaration highlighted the need for reform of multilateral institutions, including the UN Security Council, to reflect contemporary global realities.
• The summit underscored India's commitment to a partnership based on equality, mutual respect, and shared prosperity, distinguishing it from other global powers.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: The India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) is a platform for cooperation between India and African nations.
India provides Lines of Credit (LoC) as a form of development assistance.
India advocates for reforms in multilateral institutions like the UN Security Council.
mainsPoints: Examine the evolving dynamics of India-Africa relations and its strategic importance for India's foreign policy.
Compare India's approach to Africa with that of other major global powers, highlighting its unique characteristics.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing trade and investment ties between India and African countries.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from India and various African nations shaking hands at the summit.
articles.trivia: The first India-Africa Forum Summit was held in New Delhi in 2008, marking a new chapter in India's engagement with the African continent.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-08' into Orbit
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) aboard a PSLV-C59 rocket.
• EOS-08 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• The satellite is equipped with advanced optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payloads, enhancing its all-weather imaging capabilities.
• This mission further strengthens India's indigenous capabilities in remote sensing and contributes to national resource management.
• The successful launch reiterates ISRO's consistent performance in delivering critical space-based services for socio-economic development.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: EOS series satellites are Earth Observation Satellites developed by ISRO.
PSLV is a workhorse launch vehicle of ISRO, primarily used for launching Earth observation and remote sensing satellites.
SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) payloads provide all-weather, day-night imaging capabilities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's developmental goals and disaster management efforts.
Analyze India's achievements in space technology and its role in fostering self-reliance and international collaboration.
Examine the applications of remote sensing technology in various sectors such as agriculture, urban planning, and environmental monitoring.
articles.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket launching into space with the ISRO logo.
articles.trivia: ISRO holds the record for launching the most satellites in a single mission (104 satellites in 2017) using its PSLV.

articles.headline: India Unveils New National Climate Adaptation Plan for Coastal Regions
articles.explainer:
• India today launched a comprehensive 'National Climate Adaptation Plan for Coastal Regions' to enhance resilience against climate change impacts.
• The plan focuses on protecting vulnerable coastal communities, safeguarding biodiversity, and developing climate-resilient infrastructure.
• Key components include early warning systems, mangrove restoration, sea wall construction, and sustainable livelihood promotion for coastal populations.
• The initiative aligns with India's commitments under the Paris Agreement and its broader national climate action goals.
• Funding will be mobilized through a combination of national schemes, international climate finance, and public-private partnerships.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Climate change adaptation
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Coastal regions are particularly vulnerable to climate change impacts like sea-level rise and extreme weather events.
Mangroves play a crucial role in coastal protection and biodiversity.
The Paris Agreement is an international treaty on climate change adopted in 2015.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by India's coastal regions due to climate change and human activities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of different adaptation strategies, such as nature-based solutions versus hard engineering solutions, for coastal resilience.
Analyze the policy and financial mechanisms required to implement large-scale climate adaptation plans in a developing country like India.
articles.imageDescription: A coastline with mangroves and a protective sea wall, illustrating climate adaptation.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, making its coastal communities highly susceptible to climate change impacts.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission 2.0' to Expand Digital Health Ecosystem
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched 'Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) 2.0', an enhanced version aimed at expanding India's digital health infrastructure.
• ABDM 2.0 focuses on universalizing the Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) for all citizens and integrating more healthcare providers.
• New features include advanced data analytics for public health management and enhanced interoperability between various digital health platforms.
• The mission seeks to create a seamless online platform for patients to access their health records, consult doctors, and avail healthcare services.
• Data privacy and security remain paramount, with robust encryption and consent-based access mechanisms embedded in the updated framework.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem in India.
ABHA is a unique health ID for individuals under ABDM.
The mission facilitates access to digital health records and telemedicine.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of a universal digital health ecosystem in a diverse country like India.
Discuss how ABDM 2.0 can address issues of accessibility, affordability, and quality in healthcare services.
Examine the ethical and privacy concerns associated with large-scale digital health data management and the safeguards required.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital health services and an ABHA card.
articles.trivia: The original Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission was launched in September 2021 by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

articles.headline: Traditional 'Loinloom Shawl' from Nagaland Receives Geographical Indication (GI) Tag
articles.explainer:
• The traditional 'Loinloom Shawl' from Nagaland has been granted the Geographical Indication (GI) tag, recognizing its unique origin and cultural significance.
• This intricate hand-woven textile, primarily crafted by Naga women, is known for its vibrant colors, distinctive patterns, and cultural motifs.
• The GI tag will help protect the traditional knowledge and craftsmanship, preventing unauthorized use of the product's name and promoting its authentic producers.
• It is expected to boost the economic prospects of local weavers and artisans in Nagaland, enhancing their market access and preserving their heritage.
• This recognition highlights the rich textile traditions of India's Northeast and the importance of intellectual property rights for traditional crafts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India
articles.source: GI Registry / Ministry of Commerce & Industry
prelimsPoints: A GI tag is a sign used on products that have a specific geographical origin and possess qualities or a reputation due to that origin.
Nagaland is a state in Northeast India known for its diverse tribal cultures and handicrafts.
The GI tag is governed by the Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic benefits of Geographical Indication (GI) tags for traditional crafts and rural communities in India.
Analyze the role of GI tags in preserving cultural heritage, promoting local economies, and protecting indigenous knowledge.
Examine the challenges in promoting and marketing GI-tagged products from remote regions like Northeast India.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of a vibrantly colored Naga Loinloom Shawl.
articles.trivia: The 'Loinloom' is a traditional backstrap loom used by women in Northeast India, particularly in Nagaland, for weaving intricate textiles.

articles.headline: Election Commission Proposes Sweeping Reforms to Enhance Transparency in Political Funding
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) today submitted a comprehensive set of proposals to the government aimed at enhancing transparency in political funding.
• Key recommendations include mandating disclosure of all donations above a certain threshold, irrespective of the source, and strengthening audit mechanisms for political parties.
• The ECI also suggested bringing political parties under the ambit of the Right to Information (RTI) Act for greater public scrutiny.
• These reforms are intended to address concerns about opaque funding practices, black money in elections, and potential undue influence on policy-making.
• The proposals are now with the Law Ministry for consideration and potential legislative action.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Transparency and accountability and institutional and other measures
prelimsPoints: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections.
Political funding refers to the financial contributions made to political parties.
The RTI Act allows citizens to request information from public authorities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by opaque political funding practices to the integrity of electoral democracy in India.
Analyze the various electoral reforms proposed by the Election Commission over the years and their impact on Indian elections.
Evaluate the arguments for and against bringing political parties under the ambit of the Right to Information (RTI) Act.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with a magnifying glass, symbolizing transparency in elections.
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India is mentioned in Article 324 of the Constitution of India.

articles.headline: India's First Hyperloop Test Track Project Commences Construction in Andhra Pradesh
articles.explainer:
• Construction has officially commenced on India's first Hyperloop test track project in Andhra Pradesh, near Visakhapatnam.
• The project aims to develop and validate hyperloop technology for ultra-high-speed transportation of passengers and cargo.
• This initiative is a collaboration between the state government, a leading global hyperloop company, and academic institutions.
• The test track will serve as a crucial step towards potentially integrating hyperloop systems into India's future transportation network.
• Proponents highlight hyperloop's potential to revolutionize travel times, reduce congestion, and offer an environmentally friendly alternative to traditional modes.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: Hyperloop is a proposed high-speed transportation system.
It involves pods traveling at high speeds through low-pressure tubes.
The technology uses magnetic levitation to reduce friction.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of hyperloop technology to transform India's transportation landscape and its socio-economic implications.
Analyze the challenges and feasibility of implementing hyperloop systems in India, considering infrastructure, cost, and regulatory aspects.
Compare hyperloop with other high-speed transit options like bullet trains and evaluate its environmental benefits.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of a hyperloop pod traveling inside a tube.
articles.trivia: The concept of hyperloop was popularized by Elon Musk in 2013, envisioning a fifth mode of transport after planes, trains, automobiles, and boats.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases National Framework for Ethical AI Deployment in Public Services
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog today unveiled a comprehensive 'National Framework for Ethical AI Deployment in Public Services' to guide responsible AI adoption.
• The framework emphasizes principles of fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, and privacy in the design and use of AI systems by government bodies.
• It provides guidelines for data governance, algorithmic bias detection, human oversight, and stakeholder engagement in AI projects.
• The initiative aims to leverage AI's potential for improving public service delivery while mitigating risks of bias, discrimination, and misuse.
• This framework is a crucial step towards establishing India as a leader in responsible and ethical AI development and application.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is a policy think tank of the Government of India.
Ethical AI focuses on principles like fairness, transparency, and accountability.
Algorithmic bias refers to systematic and repeatable errors in an AI system due to flawed assumptions or data.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges associated with the deployment of Artificial Intelligence in public services, particularly concerning bias, privacy, and accountability.
Analyze how NITI Aayog's framework aims to address these challenges and ensure responsible AI adoption in India.
Evaluate the potential of AI to enhance governance and public service delivery while also considering the risks and necessary safeguards.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized brain with interconnected nodes, representing AI and ethical guidelines.
articles.trivia: NITI Aayog has been instrumental in formulating India's strategy for Artificial Intelligence, including publishing a 'National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence' in 2018.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Artificial Intelligence and its ethical implications

articles.headline: New Species of Freshwater Fish Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot
articles.explainer:
• A new species of freshwater fish, yet to be formally named, has been discovered in the pristine streams of the Western Ghats in Kerala.
• The discovery underscores the immense biodiversity of the Western Ghats, recognized as one of the world's 36 biodiversity hotspots.
• Researchers highlight the unique ecological conditions of the region that foster high endemism and the discovery of new species.
• The new fish species belongs to the family Cyprinidae, known for its diverse freshwater fish.
• The discovery calls for intensified conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats from threats like habitat loss and climate change.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Ecology
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a biodiversity hotspot.
Biodiversity hotspots are regions with a high level of endemic species that are under threat.
Endemism refers to species found exclusively in a particular geographical area.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species in understanding ecosystem health and guiding conservation strategies.
Examine the role of scientific research and public awareness in promoting biodiversity conservation in India.
articles.imageDescription: A newly discovered freshwater fish species swimming in a clear stream.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: Nation Remembers Rajiv Gandhi on His Death Anniversary, Focus on Contributions to Modern India
articles.explainer:
• India today observed the 34th death anniversary of former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi, who was assassinated on May 21, 1991.
• Leaders across the political spectrum paid tributes, recalling his vision for a modern, technologically advanced India.
• His tenure saw significant reforms in telecommunications, education (Navodaya Vidyalayas), and the introduction of Panchayati Raj institutions.
• Rajiv Gandhi is often credited with laying the groundwork for India's IT revolution and liberal economic policies.
• The day serves as a reminder of the challenges of terrorism and the importance of national unity.
gsSubTopics: Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues
prelimsPoints: Rajiv Gandhi served as the 6th Prime Minister of India from 1984 to 1989.
He played a key role in introducing computers and telecommunications into India.
The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendment Acts (Panchayati Raj) were conceptualized during his tenure.
mainsPoints: Analyze Rajiv Gandhi's contributions to India's technological advancement and economic liberalization.
Discuss the impact of his policies on education and grassroots democracy (Panchayati Raj) in India.
Examine the challenges he faced during his premiership, including internal dissent and regional conflicts.
articles.imageDescription: A portrait of former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi.
articles.trivia: Rajiv Gandhi was the youngest Prime Minister of India, assuming office at the age of 40.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support and Resources to UN Peacekeeping Missions
articles.explainer:
• India today reaffirmed its commitment to global peace and security by pledging enhanced support and resources to United Nations Peacekeeping Missions.
• During a high-level virtual summit, India emphasized the need for better training, equipment, and safety for peacekeepers, especially against emerging threats.
• India also called for greater representation of troop-contributing countries in the UN's decision-making processes regarding peacekeeping operations.
• With over 250,000 Indian peacekeepers having served in various UN missions since 1948, India remains one of the largest contributors of troops.
• The pledge includes specialized units and technological solutions to bolster the effectiveness of UN peacekeeping efforts worldwide.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs / UN News
prelimsPoints: India is one of the largest troop-contributing countries to UN Peacekeeping Missions.
UN Peacekeeping operations aim to maintain international peace and security.
The UN Security Council authorizes peacekeeping missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical and contemporary role in UN Peacekeeping operations and its significance for India's global standing.
Analyze the challenges faced by UN Peacekeeping Missions, including funding, mandate clarity, and safety of personnel.
Examine India's proposals for reforms in UN Peacekeeping and their potential impact on the effectiveness of these missions.
articles.imageDescription: Indian peacekeepers in blue helmets on a UN mission.
articles.trivia: India has contributed female Formed Police Units (FPU) to UN peacekeeping missions, notably in Liberia, demonstrating its commitment to gender equality in peacekeeping.

articles.headline: India Unveils 'National Water Security Mission' to Address Growing Water Scarcity
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the 'National Water Security Mission' with an ambitious goal to ensure sustainable water management and access for all citizens.
• The mission integrates various existing schemes and introduces new initiatives for water conservation, groundwater recharge, and efficient irrigation practices.
• Focus areas include inter-state river linking projects, rainwater harvesting infrastructure, and promoting water-efficient technologies in agriculture and industry.
• A key objective is to enhance water governance, promote community participation, and leverage technology for real-time monitoring of water resources.
• This comprehensive approach aims to tackle the escalating challenges of water scarcity, pollution, and climate change impacts on India's water resources.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Water resource management
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Water security refers to the capacity of a population to safeguard sustainable access to adequate quantities of acceptable quality water.
Groundwater recharge is the process by which water percolates down from the surface to replenish groundwater aquifers.
Inter-state river water disputes are addressed under Article 262 of the Indian Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional challenges India faces in achieving water security, including climate change, population growth, and unsustainable practices.
Analyze the key components of the National Water Security Mission and evaluate its potential effectiveness in addressing these challenges.
Examine the importance of community participation and inter-state cooperation in successful water resource management in India.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various water conservation methods like rainwater harvesting and dams.
articles.trivia: India, despite having 18% of the world's population, possesses only 4% of the world's freshwater resources.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meet, Focus on AI Governance and Digital Public Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• India today hosted the G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meeting, bringing together global leaders to discuss critical aspects of the digital economy.
• Key agenda items included developing common frameworks for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance, fostering digital skilling, and promoting Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• India presented its success stories with DPI, such as Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN, as models for other developing nations.
• The meeting aimed to forge consensus on cross-border data flows, cybersecurity, and ensuring inclusive access to digital technologies.
• The outcomes are expected to feed into the broader G20 Leaders' Summit, shaping global policies for a sustainable and equitable digital future.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: The G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for societal and market-scale enablement.
Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN are examples of India's DPI.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's role in shaping global discourse on the digital economy, particularly concerning AI governance and Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
Analyze the significance of DPI for developing countries and how India's model can be replicated globally.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for international cooperation in establishing norms for AI, cybersecurity, and cross-border data flows.
articles.imageDescription: A G20 logo superimposed on a digital network background.
articles.trivia: India held the G20 Presidency in 2023, where digital public infrastructure was a key focus area.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Integrated National Urban Planning Policy for Sustainable Cities
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee today submitted its report recommending an integrated National Urban Planning Policy to ensure sustainable development of Indian cities.
• The report highlighted issues such as haphazard urbanization, inadequate infrastructure, environmental degradation, and lack of citizen participation in urban governance.
• Key recommendations include promoting smart city concepts, ensuring affordable housing, improving public transport, and implementing robust waste management systems.
• The panel stressed the need for a multi-stakeholder approach involving central, state, and local bodies, along with private sector and community engagement.
• The proposed policy aims to address the challenges posed by rapid urbanization and create livable, resilient, and inclusive urban spaces.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committees examine bills and policies in detail.
Urbanization is the process by which populations shift from rural to urban areas.
Smart Cities Mission is a flagship urban development program of the Indian government.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges associated with rapid urbanization in India and the need for a comprehensive urban planning policy.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee and their potential impact on making Indian cities sustainable and livable.
Examine the role of various stakeholders, including central, state, and local governments, in effective urban governance and planning.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a modern, well-planned city with green spaces.
articles.trivia: India is projected to have 600 million people living in urban areas by 2030, making urban planning a critical challenge.

highlights: 27 Naxalites killed in a major anti-Maoist operation in Abujhmarh, Chhattisgarh, marking a significant win for security forces.
Nambala Keshava Rao, General Secretary of the outlawed CPI (Maoist) and a key strategist, passes away at 69-70.
Supreme Court upholds the constitutionality of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, providing legal certainty for India's data privacy framework.
RBI maintains the repo rate at 6.5% for the eighth consecutive time, signaling a cautious stance on inflation amidst global uncertainties.
India hosts the 7th India-Africa Forum Summit, focusing on enhanced trade, development partnerships, and multilateral institution reforms.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent anti-Naxalite operation in Abujhmarh, consider the following statements:
1. Abujhmarh is located in the Bastar district of Chhattisgarh.
2. The operation involved security forces like the District Reserve Guard (DRG) and Special Task Force (STF).
3. Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) is primarily a socio-economic issue with developmental linkages.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: Abujhmarh is located in Narayanpur district, not Bastar, though both are in Chhattisgarh and Naxal-affected. Statement 2 is correct: The operation involved DRG and STF. Statement 3 is correct: LWE is widely recognized as having deep roots in socio-economic disparities and lack of development in affected regions.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about Nambala Keshava Rao is/are correct?
1. He was the founder of the Communist Party of India (Maoist).
2. He was known by the aliases Basavaraj and Ganesh.
3. He succeeded Ganapathy as the General Secretary of CPI (Maoist) in 2018.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The CPI (Maoist) was formed in 2004 by the merger of the People's War Group and the Maoist Communist Centre of India. Nambala Keshava Rao was not its founder. Statements 2 and 3 are correct as per the news article.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023:
1. It aims to protect the digital personal data of individuals in India.
2. The Supreme Court has recently struck down its constitutionality due to privacy concerns.
3. It mandates the establishment of a Data Protection Board.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The DPDP Act, 2023, is designed to protect digital personal data. Statement 2 is incorrect: The Supreme Court *upheld* its constitutionality, it did not strike it down. Statement 3 is correct: The Act mandates the establishment of a Data Protection Board of India.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy, which of the following statements is correct?
options: (a) The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is headed by the Union Finance Minister.
(b) The primary objective of the RBI's monetary policy is to maintain high economic growth, even at the cost of inflation.
(c) The repo rate is the rate at which commercial banks lend money to the RBI.
(d) The RBI has recently maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) The RBI has recently maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Option (a) is incorrect: The MPC is headed by the RBI Governor. Option (b) is incorrect: The primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth. Option (c) is incorrect: The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks (short-term). The rate at which commercial banks lend to RBI is the reverse repo rate. Option (d) is correct: The news states the RBI reiterated its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following are key areas of cooperation discussed during the India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS)?
1. Trade and investment
2. Climate action and food security
3. Reform of multilateral institutions

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The 7th IAFS focused on deeper collaboration in trade, investment, technology transfer, capacity building, sustainable development, food security, climate action, health cooperation, and called for reform of multilateral institutions.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the government's strategy for combating Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) in India, particularly in challenging terrains like Abujhmarh. Discuss the balance between security operations and developmental initiatives. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism; various security forces and agencies and their mandate. Linkages between development and spread of extremism.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce LWE as a major internal security threat. Discuss the 'multi-pronged strategy' of security operations (like Abujhmarh clash) and developmental projects (infrastructure, welfare schemes). Highlight the challenges in balancing these two aspects. Conclude with the need for a holistic approach.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's upholding of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, marks a significant step in India's digital governance journey. Discuss the implications of this judgment for individual privacy, state surveillance, and the digital economy. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation. Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability. GS3: Basics of cyber security.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by stating the significance of the judgment. Discuss implications for individual privacy (data principal rights, consent), state surveillance (exceptions for national security), and the digital economy (compliance burden for data fiduciaries, cross-border data transfers). Highlight the balance attempted by the court and the challenges in implementation.

mainsMocks.question: India's engagement with African nations through platforms like the India-Africa Forum Summit reflects its 'Global South' strategy. Examine the strategic rationale behind India's strengthening ties with Africa and the challenges in realizing the full potential of this partnership. (200 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests. Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Define India's 'Global South' strategy. Discuss the strategic rationale: energy security, market access, UN Security Council reforms support, counterbalancing other global powers, historical ties. Challenges: competition from China, lack of sufficient financial resources, capacity building, infrastructure gaps. Conclude with mutual benefits and future prospects.

quickBytes.text: Abujhmarh is a remote, forested hilly area in Chhattisgarh, often called 'the unknown hills'.
Nambala Keshava Rao was the General Secretary of the Communist Party of India (Maoist) since 2018.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023 provides a framework for data protection in India.
The Repo Rate is the interest rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) is the official platform for India-Africa relations.
ISRO's PSLV is known as its workhorse rocket for launching Earth Observation Satellites.
The Western Ghats are one of the world's 36 biodiversity hotspots, known for high endemism.
Rajiv Gandhi is credited with ushering in the computer and telecom revolution in India.
The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body under Article 324.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems like Aadhaar and UPI.

GS1.headline: Traditional 'Loinloom Shawl' from Nagaland Receives Geographical Indication (GI) Tag
GS1.explainer:
• The traditional 'Loinloom Shawl' from Nagaland has been granted the Geographical Indication (GI) tag, recognizing its unique origin and cultural significance.
• This intricate hand-woven textile, primarily crafted by Naga women, is known for its vibrant colors, distinctive patterns, and cultural motifs.
• The GI tag will help protect the traditional knowledge and craftsmanship, preventing unauthorized use of the product's name and promoting its authentic producers.
• It is expected to boost the economic prospects of local weavers and artisans in Nagaland, enhancing their market access and preserving their heritage.
• This recognition highlights the rich textile traditions of India's Northeast and the importance of intellectual property rights for traditional crafts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India
GS1.source: GI Registry / Ministry of Commerce & Industry
prelimsPoints: A GI tag is a sign used on products that have a specific geographical origin and possess qualities or a reputation due to that origin.
Nagaland is a state in Northeast India known for its diverse tribal cultures and handicrafts.
The GI tag is governed by the Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic benefits of Geographical Indication (GI) tags for traditional crafts and rural communities in India.
Analyze the role of GI tags in preserving cultural heritage, promoting local economies, and protecting indigenous knowledge.
Examine the challenges in promoting and marketing GI-tagged products from remote regions like Northeast India.
GS1.imageDescription: A close-up of a vibrantly colored Naga Loinloom Shawl.
GS1.trivia: The 'Loinloom' is a traditional backstrap loom used by women in Northeast India, particularly in Nagaland, for weaving intricate textiles.

GS1.headline: Nation Remembers Rajiv Gandhi on His Death Anniversary, Focus on Contributions to Modern India
GS1.explainer:
• India today observed the 34th death anniversary of former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi, who was assassinated on May 21, 1991.
• Leaders across the political spectrum paid tributes, recalling his vision for a modern, technologically advanced India.
• His tenure saw significant reforms in telecommunications, education (Navodaya Vidyalayas), and the introduction of Panchayati Raj institutions.
• Rajiv Gandhi is often credited with laying the groundwork for India's IT revolution and liberal economic policies.
• The day serves as a reminder of the challenges of terrorism and the importance of national unity.
gsSubTopics: Post-independence consolidation and reorganization within the country
Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues
prelimsPoints: Rajiv Gandhi served as the 6th Prime Minister of India from 1984 to 1989.
He played a key role in introducing computers and telecommunications into India.
The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendment Acts (Panchayati Raj) were conceptualized during his tenure.
mainsPoints: Analyze Rajiv Gandhi's contributions to India's technological advancement and economic liberalization.
Discuss the impact of his policies on education and grassroots democracy (Panchayati Raj) in India.
Examine the challenges he faced during his premiership, including internal dissent and regional conflicts.
GS1.imageDescription: A portrait of former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi.
GS1.trivia: Rajiv Gandhi was the youngest Prime Minister of India, assuming office at the age of 40.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutionality of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• Several petitions had challenged the law on grounds of privacy concerns, state surveillance powers, and potential impact on freedom of speech.
• The Court ruled that the law strikes a reasonable balance between an individual's right to privacy and legitimate state interests, including national security and public order.
• Key provisions regarding cross-border data transfers and the establishment of the Data Protection Board were specifically scrutinized and affirmed.
• This landmark judgment provides legal certainty for the implementation of India's comprehensive data protection framework.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability
Citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures
Fundamental Rights
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, is India's comprehensive data protection law.
The Supreme Court exercises powers of judicial review over parliamentary legislation.
The Right to Privacy is protected under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
mainsPoints: Analyze the balance between individual privacy rights and state interests in the context of the DPDP Act, 2023.
Discuss the implications of the DPDP Act for businesses and technology companies operating in India.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in upholding fundamental rights while also acknowledging the need for national security and public order.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of digital data flowing securely with a Supreme Court emblem in the background.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution, laying the groundwork for the DPDP Act.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and data protection

GS2.headline: 7th India-Africa Forum Summit Concludes, Focus on Enhanced Trade and Development Partnerships
GS2.explainer:
• The 7th India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) concluded today, emphasizing deeper collaboration in trade, investment, technology transfer, and capacity building.
• Leaders from India and 54 African nations discussed strategies for sustainable development, food security, climate action, and health cooperation.
• India pledged increased Lines of Credit and grants for development projects across Africa, reinforcing its 'Global South' outreach.
• A joint declaration highlighted the need for reform of multilateral institutions, including the UN Security Council, to reflect contemporary global realities.
• The summit underscored India's commitment to a partnership based on equality, mutual respect, and shared prosperity, distinguishing it from other global powers.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: The India-Africa Forum Summit (IAFS) is a platform for cooperation between India and African nations.
India provides Lines of Credit (LoC) as a form of development assistance.
India advocates for reforms in multilateral institutions like the UN Security Council.
mainsPoints: Examine the evolving dynamics of India-Africa relations and its strategic importance for India's foreign policy.
Compare India's approach to Africa with that of other major global powers, highlighting its unique characteristics.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing trade and investment ties between India and African countries.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders from India and various African nations shaking hands at the summit.
GS2.trivia: The first India-Africa Forum Summit was held in New Delhi in 2008, marking a new chapter in India's engagement with the African continent.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission 2.0' to Expand Digital Health Ecosystem
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched 'Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) 2.0', an enhanced version aimed at expanding India's digital health infrastructure.
• ABDM 2.0 focuses on universalizing the Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) for all citizens and integrating more healthcare providers.
• New features include advanced data analytics for public health management and enhanced interoperability between various digital health platforms.
• The mission seeks to create a seamless online platform for patients to access their health records, consult doctors, and avail healthcare services.
• Data privacy and security remain paramount, with robust encryption and consent-based access mechanisms embedded in the updated framework.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem in India.
ABHA is a unique health ID for individuals under ABDM.
The mission facilitates access to digital health records and telemedicine.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential benefits and challenges of a universal digital health ecosystem in a diverse country like India.
Discuss how ABDM 2.0 can address issues of accessibility, affordability, and quality in healthcare services.
Examine the ethical and privacy concerns associated with large-scale digital health data management and the safeguards required.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital health services and an ABHA card.
GS2.trivia: The original Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission was launched in September 2021 by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

GS2.headline: Election Commission Proposes Sweeping Reforms to Enhance Transparency in Political Funding
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) today submitted a comprehensive set of proposals to the government aimed at enhancing transparency in political funding.
• Key recommendations include mandating disclosure of all donations above a certain threshold, irrespective of the source, and strengthening audit mechanisms for political parties.
• The ECI also suggested bringing political parties under the ambit of the Right to Information (RTI) Act for greater public scrutiny.
• These reforms are intended to address concerns about opaque funding practices, black money in elections, and potential undue influence on policy-making.
• The proposals are now with the Law Ministry for consideration and potential legislative action.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Transparency and accountability and institutional and other measures
prelimsPoints: The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections.
Political funding refers to the financial contributions made to political parties.
The RTI Act allows citizens to request information from public authorities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by opaque political funding practices to the integrity of electoral democracy in India.
Analyze the various electoral reforms proposed by the Election Commission over the years and their impact on Indian elections.
Evaluate the arguments for and against bringing political parties under the ambit of the Right to Information (RTI) Act.
GS2.imageDescription: A ballot box with a magnifying glass, symbolizing transparency in elections.
GS2.trivia: The Election Commission of India is mentioned in Article 324 of the Constitution of India.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support and Resources to UN Peacekeeping Missions
GS2.explainer:
• India today reaffirmed its commitment to global peace and security by pledging enhanced support and resources to United Nations Peacekeeping Missions.
• During a high-level virtual summit, India emphasized the need for better training, equipment, and safety for peacekeepers, especially against emerging threats.
• India also called for greater representation of troop-contributing countries in the UN's decision-making processes regarding peacekeeping operations.
• With over 250,000 Indian peacekeepers having served in various UN missions since 1948, India remains one of the largest contributors of troops.
• The pledge includes specialized units and technological solutions to bolster the effectiveness of UN peacekeeping efforts worldwide.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
GS2.source: Ministry of External Affairs / UN News
prelimsPoints: India is one of the largest troop-contributing countries to UN Peacekeeping Missions.
UN Peacekeeping operations aim to maintain international peace and security.
The UN Security Council authorizes peacekeeping missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's historical and contemporary role in UN Peacekeeping operations and its significance for India's global standing.
Analyze the challenges faced by UN Peacekeeping Missions, including funding, mandate clarity, and safety of personnel.
Examine India's proposals for reforms in UN Peacekeeping and their potential impact on the effectiveness of these missions.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian peacekeepers in blue helmets on a UN mission.
GS2.trivia: India has contributed female Formed Police Units (FPU) to UN peacekeeping missions, notably in Liberia, demonstrating its commitment to gender equality in peacekeeping.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meet, Focus on AI Governance and Digital Public Infrastructure
GS2.explainer:
• India today hosted the G20 Digital Economy Ministers' Meeting, bringing together global leaders to discuss critical aspects of the digital economy.
• Key agenda items included developing common frameworks for Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance, fostering digital skilling, and promoting Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• India presented its success stories with DPI, such as Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN, as models for other developing nations.
• The meeting aimed to forge consensus on cross-border data flows, cybersecurity, and ensuring inclusive access to digital technologies.
• The outcomes are expected to feed into the broader G20 Leaders' Summit, shaping global policies for a sustainable and equitable digital future.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: The G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for societal and market-scale enablement.
Aadhaar, UPI, and CoWIN are examples of India's DPI.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's role in shaping global discourse on the digital economy, particularly concerning AI governance and Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
Analyze the significance of DPI for developing countries and how India's model can be replicated globally.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for international cooperation in establishing norms for AI, cybersecurity, and cross-border data flows.
GS2.imageDescription: A G20 logo superimposed on a digital network background.
GS2.trivia: India held the G20 Presidency in 2023, where digital public infrastructure was a key focus area.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Integrated National Urban Planning Policy for Sustainable Cities
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee today submitted its report recommending an integrated National Urban Planning Policy to ensure sustainable development of Indian cities.
• The report highlighted issues such as haphazard urbanization, inadequate infrastructure, environmental degradation, and lack of citizen participation in urban governance.
• Key recommendations include promoting smart city concepts, ensuring affordable housing, improving public transport, and implementing robust waste management systems.
• The panel stressed the need for a multi-stakeholder approach involving central, state, and local bodies, along with private sector and community engagement.
• The proposed policy aims to address the challenges posed by rapid urbanization and create livable, resilient, and inclusive urban spaces.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committees examine bills and policies in detail.
Urbanization is the process by which populations shift from rural to urban areas.
Smart Cities Mission is a flagship urban development program of the Indian government.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges associated with rapid urbanization in India and the need for a comprehensive urban planning policy.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee and their potential impact on making Indian cities sustainable and livable.
Examine the role of various stakeholders, including central, state, and local governments, in effective urban governance and planning.
GS2.imageDescription: An aerial view of a modern, well-planned city with green spaces.
GS2.trivia: India is projected to have 600 million people living in urban areas by 2030, making urban planning a critical challenge.

GS3.headline: Major Anti-Maoist Operation in Abujhmarh, Chhattisgarh; 27 Naxalites Killed
GS3.explainer:
• A significant anti-Naxal operation in the Abujhmarh region of Narayanpur district, Chhattisgarh, resulted in the killing of 27 Naxalites.
• The operation was a joint effort by security forces, including the District Reserve Guard (DRG) and Special Task Force (STF).
• Abujhmarh is known as a Maoist stronghold and a challenging terrain for security forces due to its dense forests and lack of infrastructure.
• This incident marks one of the largest single-day casualties for Naxalites in recent times, indicating intensified efforts by the state to curb left-wing extremism.
• The operation is part of the ongoing strategy to dismantle Maoist networks and establish state control in previously inaccessible areas.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Abujhmarh is located in Narayanpur district, Chhattisgarh.
DRG (District Reserve Guard) and STF (Special Task Force) are key forces involved in anti-Naxal operations.
Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) is a significant internal security challenge in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the effectiveness of security-centric approaches versus development-centric approaches in combating LWE.
Analyze the geographical challenges and strategic importance of regions like Abujhmarh in the context of LWE.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to the persistence of LWE and government strategies to address them.
GS3.imageDescription: Security forces conducting operations in a dense forest area of Chhattisgarh.
GS3.trivia: Abujhmarh, literally meaning 'the unknown hills', is one of the last remaining unmapped and uncharted areas in India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Linkages between development and spread of extremism

GS3.headline: Top Maoist Leader Nambala Keshava Rao, General Secretary of CPI (Maoist), Passes Away
GS3.explainer:
• Nambala Keshava Rao, also known as Basavaraj or Ganesh, the General Secretary of the outlawed Communist Party of India (Maoist), has passed away at the age of 69-70.
• He assumed the leadership of the CPI (Maoist) in 2018, succeeding Ganapathy, and was considered a key strategist and military planner for the outfit.
• Rao was a former engineer and was known for his expertise in designing and manufacturing improvised explosive devices (IEDs).
• His death is expected to create a significant leadership vacuum within the top echelons of the Maoist organization.
• Security agencies view this as a potential setback for the LWE movement, possibly impacting its operational capabilities and future strategies.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: Nambala Keshava Rao was the General Secretary of the CPI (Maoist).
He was also known by the aliases Basavaraj and Ganesh.
He succeeded Ganapathy as the head of the outfit in 2018.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential impact of leadership changes on the operational effectiveness and ideological direction of LWE groups.
Discuss the role of key leaders in sustaining and expanding Naxalite movements.
Examine the challenges faced by security agencies in identifying and neutralizing top Maoist leadership.
GS3.imageDescription: A blurred file photo of Nambala Keshava Rao.
GS3.trivia: Nambala Keshava Rao was reportedly instrumental in modernizing the Maoists' arsenal and tactics, particularly in the use of explosives.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Linkages between development and spread of extremism

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Signals Cautious Stance on Inflation
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the eighth consecutive time.
• The MPC emphasized its commitment to bringing inflation within the target band of 4% while supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global uncertainties and domestic food price pressures as key risks to the inflation outlook.
• The central bank reiterated its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating that future rate cuts are not imminent.
• Analysts expect the RBI to remain watchful of evolving economic indicators, particularly monsoon performance and crude oil prices, before considering any policy changes.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: The Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is responsible for setting the policy interest rates.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth in the current global and domestic environment.
Analyze the impact of an unchanged repo rate on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in managing liquidity and inflationary pressures.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai with currency notes in the foreground.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Tools and objectives of monetary policy

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-08' into Orbit
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) aboard a PSLV-C59 rocket.
• EOS-08 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• The satellite is equipped with advanced optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payloads, enhancing its all-weather imaging capabilities.
• This mission further strengthens India's indigenous capabilities in remote sensing and contributes to national resource management.
• The successful launch reiterates ISRO's consistent performance in delivering critical space-based services for socio-economic development.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: EOS series satellites are Earth Observation Satellites developed by ISRO.
PSLV is a workhorse launch vehicle of ISRO, primarily used for launching Earth observation and remote sensing satellites.
SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) payloads provide all-weather, day-night imaging capabilities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's developmental goals and disaster management efforts.
Analyze India's achievements in space technology and its role in fostering self-reliance and international collaboration.
Examine the applications of remote sensing technology in various sectors such as agriculture, urban planning, and environmental monitoring.
GS3.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket launching into space with the ISRO logo.
GS3.trivia: ISRO holds the record for launching the most satellites in a single mission (104 satellites in 2017) using its PSLV.

GS3.headline: India Unveils New National Climate Adaptation Plan for Coastal Regions
GS3.explainer:
• India today launched a comprehensive 'National Climate Adaptation Plan for Coastal Regions' to enhance resilience against climate change impacts.
• The plan focuses on protecting vulnerable coastal communities, safeguarding biodiversity, and developing climate-resilient infrastructure.
• Key components include early warning systems, mangrove restoration, sea wall construction, and sustainable livelihood promotion for coastal populations.
• The initiative aligns with India's commitments under the Paris Agreement and its broader national climate action goals.
• Funding will be mobilized through a combination of national schemes, international climate finance, and public-private partnerships.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Climate change adaptation
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Coastal regions are particularly vulnerable to climate change impacts like sea-level rise and extreme weather events.
Mangroves play a crucial role in coastal protection and biodiversity.
The Paris Agreement is an international treaty on climate change adopted in 2015.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced by India's coastal regions due to climate change and human activities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of different adaptation strategies, such as nature-based solutions versus hard engineering solutions, for coastal resilience.
Analyze the policy and financial mechanisms required to implement large-scale climate adaptation plans in a developing country like India.
GS3.imageDescription: A coastline with mangroves and a protective sea wall, illustrating climate adaptation.
GS3.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, making its coastal communities highly susceptible to climate change impacts.

GS3.headline: India's First Hyperloop Test Track Project Commences Construction in Andhra Pradesh
GS3.explainer:
• Construction has officially commenced on India's first Hyperloop test track project in Andhra Pradesh, near Visakhapatnam.
• The project aims to develop and validate hyperloop technology for ultra-high-speed transportation of passengers and cargo.
• This initiative is a collaboration between the state government, a leading global hyperloop company, and academic institutions.
• The test track will serve as a crucial step towards potentially integrating hyperloop systems into India's future transportation network.
• Proponents highlight hyperloop's potential to revolutionize travel times, reduce congestion, and offer an environmentally friendly alternative to traditional modes.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: Hyperloop is a proposed high-speed transportation system.
It involves pods traveling at high speeds through low-pressure tubes.
The technology uses magnetic levitation to reduce friction.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of hyperloop technology to transform India's transportation landscape and its socio-economic implications.
Analyze the challenges and feasibility of implementing hyperloop systems in India, considering infrastructure, cost, and regulatory aspects.
Compare hyperloop with other high-speed transit options like bullet trains and evaluate its environmental benefits.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of a hyperloop pod traveling inside a tube.
GS3.trivia: The concept of hyperloop was popularized by Elon Musk in 2013, envisioning a fifth mode of transport after planes, trains, automobiles, and boats.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Releases National Framework for Ethical AI Deployment in Public Services
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog today unveiled a comprehensive 'National Framework for Ethical AI Deployment in Public Services' to guide responsible AI adoption.
• The framework emphasizes principles of fairness, accountability, transparency, safety, and privacy in the design and use of AI systems by government bodies.
• It provides guidelines for data governance, algorithmic bias detection, human oversight, and stakeholder engagement in AI projects.
• The initiative aims to leverage AI's potential for improving public service delivery while mitigating risks of bias, discrimination, and misuse.
• This framework is a crucial step towards establishing India as a leader in responsible and ethical AI development and application.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is a policy think tank of the Government of India.
Ethical AI focuses on principles like fairness, transparency, and accountability.
Algorithmic bias refers to systematic and repeatable errors in an AI system due to flawed assumptions or data.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges associated with the deployment of Artificial Intelligence in public services, particularly concerning bias, privacy, and accountability.
Analyze how NITI Aayog's framework aims to address these challenges and ensure responsible AI adoption in India.
Evaluate the potential of AI to enhance governance and public service delivery while also considering the risks and necessary safeguards.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized brain with interconnected nodes, representing AI and ethical guidelines.
GS3.trivia: NITI Aayog has been instrumental in formulating India's strategy for Artificial Intelligence, including publishing a 'National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence' in 2018.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Artificial Intelligence and its ethical implications

GS3.headline: New Species of Freshwater Fish Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot
GS3.explainer:
• A new species of freshwater fish, yet to be formally named, has been discovered in the pristine streams of the Western Ghats in Kerala.
• The discovery underscores the immense biodiversity of the Western Ghats, recognized as one of the world's 36 biodiversity hotspots.
• Researchers highlight the unique ecological conditions of the region that foster high endemism and the discovery of new species.
• The new fish species belongs to the family Cyprinidae, known for its diverse freshwater fish.
• The discovery calls for intensified conservation efforts to protect the fragile ecosystems of the Western Ghats from threats like habitat loss and climate change.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Ecology
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a biodiversity hotspot.
Biodiversity hotspots are regions with a high level of endemic species that are under threat.
Endemism refers to species found exclusively in a particular geographical area.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species in understanding ecosystem health and guiding conservation strategies.
Examine the role of scientific research and public awareness in promoting biodiversity conservation in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly discovered freshwater fish species swimming in a clear stream.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

GS3.headline: India Unveils 'National Water Security Mission' to Address Growing Water Scarcity
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the 'National Water Security Mission' with an ambitious goal to ensure sustainable water management and access for all citizens.
• The mission integrates various existing schemes and introduces new initiatives for water conservation, groundwater recharge, and efficient irrigation practices.
• Focus areas include inter-state river linking projects, rainwater harvesting infrastructure, and promoting water-efficient technologies in agriculture and industry.
• A key objective is to enhance water governance, promote community participation, and leverage technology for real-time monitoring of water resources.
• This comprehensive approach aims to tackle the escalating challenges of water scarcity, pollution, and climate change impacts on India's water resources.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Water resource management
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Water security refers to the capacity of a population to safeguard sustainable access to adequate quantities of acceptable quality water.
Groundwater recharge is the process by which water percolates down from the surface to replenish groundwater aquifers.
Inter-state river water disputes are addressed under Article 262 of the Indian Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-dimensional challenges India faces in achieving water security, including climate change, population growth, and unsustainable practices.
Analyze the key components of the National Water Security Mission and evaluate its potential effectiveness in addressing these challenges.
Examine the importance of community participation and inter-state cooperation in successful water resource management in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various water conservation methods like rainwater harvesting and dams.
GS3.trivia: India, despite having 18% of the world's population, possesses only 4% of the world's freshwater resources.

sources: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
